text:
Once upon a quiet coastal town lived a shy girl named Lucy. She spent her days wandering the shorelines searching for seashells. Her favorite collection comprised tiny pink seashells, rumored to have feelings just like all living things.

Her best friend was Max, a confident boy who loved adventure. Every day after school, they would race along the waves, discovering new shell beds and sharing stories at the pier. Lucy admiringly looked up to Max.

One autumn afternoon, while rummulating on their latest discovery—a vast bed of glittering olive shells—they heard an unusual noise. It wasn’t a bird. It wasn’t a dolphin. Intrigued, they cautiously approached a peculiar, odd-looking stone statue at the end of the pier.

A flock of colorful gulls suddenly swooped down, chasing something out into the waves. Max squinted harder. Something moved! They stood motionless, eyes wide, as a graceful whale swam right into their view—a majestic creature gl
